If the user wants a specific academic_year or semester, add a filter on course_sections.academic_year and/or course_sections.semester_type.","All referenced tables and columns were discovered and confirmed during schema preflight in this session; do not assume other columns exist."]},"attempt":2,"maxAttempts":4,"previousAttempt":{"sql":"SELECT p.id AS professor_id,
       (p.first_name || ' ' || p.last_name) AS professor_name,
       ROUND(AVG(ce.professor_rating)::numeric, 2) AS avg_professor_rating,
       COUNT(ce.id) AS num_evaluations
FROM public.course_evaluations ce
JOIN public.enrollments e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.professors p ON cs.professor_id = p.id
WHERE ce.professor_rating IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
ORDER BY avg_professor_rating DESC, num_evaluations DESC, professor_name ASC
LIMIT 10;","validationIssues":["No minimum-evaluation threshold applied — results may include professors with very few evaluations (e.g., sample shows 2); consider filtering by num_evaluations to avoid small-sample bias.","Y-axis domain is fixed to [0,5]; if the rating scale differs or you prefer automatic scaling, remove or adjust this domain.","Professor name is produced by simple concatenation (first_name || ' ' || last_name); if any name parts are NULL or you need a different name format, handle that in SQL.","Vega-Lite sort uses op:'max' on avg_professor_rating while SQL already orders results; this is not harmful but redundant — ensure visualization rendering preserves SQL ordering if relying on it."]},"historySummary":"Attempt 1: passed","latest":{"sqlDraft":{"reasoning":"Preflight confirmed the required tables and columns and validated the join path (course_evaluations -> enrollments -> course_sections -> professors).        COUNT(ce.id) AS num_evaluations
FROM public.course_evaluations ce

JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.professors p ON cs.professor_id = p.id
WHERE ce.professor_rating IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
ORDER BY avg_professor_rating DESC, num_evaluations DESC, professor_name ASC
LIMIT 10;","assumptions":["The join path course_evaluations.enrollment_id -> enrollments.id -> course_sections.id -> course_sections.professor_id -> professors.id is valid (confirmed in preflight).","Use course_evaluations.professor_rating as the metric to average; NULL ratings are excluded.","professor_name is first_name + ' ' + last_name.","Ties are resolved by higher num_evaluations, then professor_name ascending.","No additional semester/year filtering or minimum-evaluation threshold applied; add filters if required by the user."]},"execution":{"ok":true,"sql":"SELECT p.id AS professor_id,

visualization.title: Top 10 Professors by Average Student Professor Rating

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT p.id AS professor_id,
       (p.first_name || ' ' || p.last_name) AS professor_name,
       ROUND(AVG(ce.professor_rating)::numeric, 2) AS avg_professor_rating,
       COUNT(ce.id) AS num_evaluations
FROM public.course_evaluations ce
JOIN public.enrollments e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.professors p ON cs.professor_id = p.id
WHERE ce.professor_rating IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
ORDER BY avg_professor_rating DESC, num_evaluations DESC, professor_name ASC
LIMIT 10;

issues: No minimum-evaluation threshold applied — results may include professors with very few evaluations (e.g., sample shows 2); consider filtering by num_evaluations to avoid small-sample bias.
Y-axis domain is fixed to [0,5]; if the rating scale differs or you prefer automatic scaling, remove or adjust this domain.
Professor name is produced by simple concatenation (first_name || ' ' || last_name); if any name parts are NULL or you need a different name format, handle that in SQL.
Vega-Lite sort uses op:'max' on avg_professor_rating while SQL already orders results; this is not harmful but redundant — ensure visualization rendering preserves SQL ordering if relying on it.

visualization.title: Top 10 Professors by Average Student Professor Rating

result.sql:
SELECT p.id AS professor_id,
       (p.first_name || ' ' || p.last_name) AS professor_name,
       ROUND(AVG(ce.professor_rating)::numeric, 2) AS avg_professor_rating,
       COUNT(ce.id) AS num_evaluations
FROM public.course_evaluations ce
JOIN public.enrollments e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.professors p ON cs.professor_id = p.id
WHERE ce.professor_rating IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
ORDER BY avg_professor_rating DESC, num_evaluations DESC, professor_name ASC
LIMIT 10;

issues: No minimum-evaluation threshold applied — results may include professors with very few evaluations (e.g., sample shows 2); consider filtering by num_evaluations to avoid small-sample bias.
Y-axis domain is fixed to [0,5]; if the rating scale differs or you prefer automatic scaling, remove or adjust this domain.
Professor name is produced by simple concatenation (first_name || ' ' || last_name); if any name parts are NULL or you need a different name format, handle that in SQL.
Vega-Lite sort uses op:'max' on avg_professor_rating while SQL already orders results; this is not harmful but redundant — ensure visualization rendering preserves SQL ordering if relying on it.
